title: Diabetic foot surgical review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports documenting age-appropriate review, bilateral skin and sensory examination, perfusion, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot features, and clinician-entered risk classification without calculating it.
examination.rationale: Supports bilateral skin, sensory, perfusion, ulcer, callus, infection, deformity and clinician risk documentation for diabetic foot surgical review; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting age-appropriate review, bilateral skin and sensory examination, perfusion, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot features, and clinician-entered risk classification without calculating it.

source.title: Diabetic foot problems: prevention and management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.6 — frequency, bilateral foot examination, and clinician risk stratification

referral.text: Supports documenting completed foot protection assessment, vascular or biomechanical review, clinician-selected reassessment, information already provided, and an entered follow-up plan.
referral.rationale: Supports completed specialist assessment, reassessment and information documentation for diabetic foot surgical review; no decision is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports documenting completed foot protection assessment, vascular or biomechanical review, clinician-selected reassessment, information already provided, and an entered follow-up plan.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Ulcer measurements, vascular studies, imaging, microbiology, diagnosis, debridement, offloading, antibiotics, revascularization, surgery, urgency and follow-up remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for diabetic foot surgical review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
